policy_rows.label: Per-manifest ordered partition
policy_rows.strict_order_scope: All messages for one manifest must preserve stage or evidence-binding order; cross-manifest order is intentionally unconstrained.
policy_rows.concurrency_rule: One active consumer claim per partition key at a time; horizontal scale comes from many manifests, not from reordering one manifest.
policy_rows.backlog_hazard: A poison message can stall one manifest lane, so dead-letter posture must unblock later work for other manifests without skipping durable truth.

source_refs.rationale: ADR-002 fixed the broker as a transport-only fabric fed by transactional outbox and inbox receipts, explicitly rebuildable from durable truth.
The runtime topology names a queue or broker for outbox and worker coordination while forbidding it from becoming legal truth.
Queue backlog rebuild must come from durable outbox, inbox, interaction, workflow, and audit truth rather than broker history.
Outbox messages, queues, read models, and general logs may only carry opaque refs and basis hashes, never raw tokens or secrets.
Inbound callbacks and worker results must enter through authenticated transactional inbox and dedupe layers before any state transition.

source_refs.source_heading_or_logical_block: 9.4 Request identity, idempotency, and ingress proof
source_refs.rationale: Authority callbacks and recovery reads must preserve request lineage, canonical ingress receipts, and duplicate suppression before mutation.
The dependency register marks the broker as MVP-required but still unresolved as a procurement or platform choice.
Object-store notifications already freeze logical channel names for scan, validation, evidence binding, preview, export, quarantine, retention, and restore flows.
Delivery callbacks remain evidence-only transport inputs and must not upgrade workflow truth directly.
Scheduler and retry work must preserve durable dedupe tuples rather than deriving legality from worker-local retry memory.

notes: This keeps one manifest from becoming a distributed race between worker instances.

policy_rows.label: Per-upload-session ordered partition
policy_rows.strict_order_scope: Upload-session lifecycle events stay ordered per session so scan, validation, and preview registration cannot overtake one another.
policy_rows.concurrency_rule: Independent upload sessions may process concurrently; one upload session remains serial at the broker lane.
policy_rows.backlog_hazard: Long-running scan or validation work must not block unrelated upload sessions or prompt unsafe key reuse.

Object-store notifications already freeze logical channel names for scan, validation, evidence binding, preview, export, quarantine, retention, and restore flows.
Delivery callbacks remain evidence-only transport inputs and must not upgrade workflow truth directly.
Scheduler and retry work must preserve durable dedupe tuples rather than deriving legality from worker-local retry memory.

notes: This matches the upload recovery law that a session keeps one governed identity across reconnect and retry.

policy_rows.label: Per-authority-interaction ordered partition
policy_rows.strict_order_scope: One authority interaction preserves send, poll, and retry order under a single interaction ref.
policy_rows.concurrency_rule: Independent authority interactions may run concurrently, but one interaction cannot have overlapping active send or poll claims.
policy_rows.backlog_hazard: A stuck interaction must not permit blind resend or poll floods that outrun the durable reconciliation contract.

Scheduler and retry work must preserve durable dedupe tuples rather than deriving legality from worker-local retry memory.

notes: This row keeps request-backed authority recovery deterministic after queue or worker loss.

policy_rows.label: Authority delivery dedupe partition
policy_rows.strict_order_scope: Ingress processing serializes per provider-delivery identity until one canonical receipt and outcome exist.
policy_rows.concurrency_rule: Different delivery identities may normalize in parallel, but one dedupe key must never have two concurrent mutation-capable consumers.
policy_rows.backlog_hazard: Provider duplicate storms can stall one dedupe key, so canonical receipt collapse is mandatory before higher-cost normalization repeats.

source_refs.rationale: ADR-002 fixed the broker as a transport-only fabric fed by transactional outbox and inbox receipts, explicitly rebuildable from durable truth.
The runtime topology names a queue or broker for outbox and worker coordination while forbidding it from becoming legal truth.

source_refs.source_heading_or_logical_block: 9.4 Request identity, idempotency, and ingress proof
source_refs.rationale: Authority callbacks and recovery reads must preserve request lineage, canonical ingress receipts, and duplicate suppression before mutation.
The dependency register marks the broker as MVP-required but still unresolved as a procurement or platform choice.
Object-store notifications already freeze logical channel names for scan, validation, evidence binding, preview, export, quarantine, retention, and restore flows.
Delivery callbacks remain evidence-only transport inputs and must not upgrade workflow truth directly.
Scheduler and retry work must preserve durable dedupe tuples rather than deriving legality from worker-local retry memory.

notes: This is the key transport boundary that keeps provider callback duplication from becoming legal-state duplication.

policy_rows.label: Per-delivery-binding partition
policy_rows.strict_order_scope: Evidence append order is preserved per delivery binding or governance binding only; global order is unnecessary.
policy_rows.concurrency_rule: Delivery and governance work may fan out freely across bindings but should serialize within one binding identity.
policy_rows.backlog_hazard: Provider retries or webhook duplicates can flood one binding, so the inbox ledger must absorb duplicates without generating duplicate evidence.

source_refs.rationale: Authority callbacks and recovery reads must preserve request lineage, canonical ingress receipts, and duplicate suppression before mutation.
The dependency register marks the broker as MVP-required but still unresolved as a procurement or platform choice.
Object-store notifications already freeze logical channel names for scan, validation, evidence binding, preview, export, quarantine, retention, and restore flows.
Delivery callbacks remain evidence-only transport inputs and must not upgrade workflow truth directly.
Scheduler and retry work must preserve durable dedupe tuples rather than deriving legality from worker-local retry memory.

notes: The binding hash is a safer ordering dimension than recipient address or export filename alone.

policy_rows.label: Per-checkpoint restore partition
policy_rows.strict_order_scope: Restore indexing stays ordered per checkpoint and candidate identity because reopen gates are checkpoint-bound.
policy_rows.concurrency_rule: Different checkpoints may index concurrently; one checkpoint lane remains serial until evidence and reopen gates settle.
policy_rows.backlog_hazard: A broken restore-index task must block only its checkpoint lane and keep reopen law explicit rather than losing evidence in a broad retry storm.

typed_gaps: Provider-specific throughput quotas per partition or session are deferred until the broker family is selected.
notes: Ordering is intentionally per durable identity boundary, never one global queue-wide order.
